summary: Large assembly-organization PR that removed the monolithic asm/MetroTRK.s file and split MetroTRK into named assembly files matching the intended library/file boundaries. The author also fixed a compilation typo from the previous PR and named nearly every MetroTRK function, including functions missing from existing symbol maps. Linker script and asmdiff support were updated to account for the split. Evidence is strongest for file splitting, symbol naming, and linker integration; there were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 6175-line monolithic asm/MetroTRK.s file and replaced it with many smaller MetroTRK assembly files.

Added dedicated MetroTRK-related assembly units such as AmcExi2Stubs.s, DebuggerDriver.s, msghndlr.s, targimpl.s, msgbuf.s, dolphin_trk_glue.s, mpc_7xx_603e.s, nubevent.s, dolphin_trk.s, serpoll.s, nubinit.s, mainloop.s, main_TRK.s, mem_TRK.s, dispatch.s, targcont.s, msg.s, and flush_cache.s.
Named every MetroTRK file according to the author, while correcting file splits where the linkermap was wrong.
Named nearly every MetroTRK function according to the PR body, including functions that were not previously present in symbol maps.
Left one function unsplit: the author described it as a stub function at the end of DebuggerDriver that would not link even with FORCEACTIVE and FORCEFILES.
Updated ldscript.lcf to reflect the new split assembly files.
Updated asmdiff.sh, likely so diff tooling could handle the renamed/split MetroTRK layout.
Fixed a compilation issue caused by a typo introduced in the previous PR.
The diff excerpt shows AmcExi2Stubs.s defining stubbed EXI2/AMC symbols including EXI2_Init, EXI2_EnableInterrupts, EXI2_Poll, EXI2_ReadN, EXI2_WriteN, EXI2_Reserve, EXI2_Unreserve, and AMC_IsStub.
smart_moves: Split a large monolithic assembly file into SDK-like file units before deeper decompilation, making later C translation and symbol work more navigable.
Did not blindly trust the linkermap: the author explicitly corrected file boundaries where the linkermap had wrong splits.
Preserved matching/linkability constraints by documenting the one stub function that could not be split even with FORCEACTIVE and FORCEFILES.
Kept stub behavior explicit in AmcExi2Stubs.s with small named functions returning fixed values or immediately returning, which makes later matching/decomp work easier to reason about.
Updated linker-script/tooling files alongside assembly movement, avoiding a split that only reorganized source files but failed at build integration.
decomp_lessons: For vendor/runtime blobs like MetroTRK, first recover credible file boundaries and symbol names; this creates a reusable scaffold for later decompilation.
Linker maps can be useful but should be treated as evidence, not absolute truth; verify splits against actual link behavior.
When an assembly function cannot be moved because of FORCEACTIVE/FORCEFILES/linking behavior, record that exception near the PR/change so future workers do not rediscover the same failure.
A successful asm split should include synchronized updates to ldscript.lcf and local diff/build tooling.
Stub files are worth isolating and naming even when they contain only blr or constant-return functions, because they clarify external interface points.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK files were named after apparent SDK/module names such as DebuggerDriver, msghndlr, targimpl, msgbuf, dolphin_trk_glue, mpc_7xx_603e, nubevent, serpoll, nubinit, mainloop, main_TRK, mem_TRK, dispatch, targcont, msg, and flush_cache.

EXI2 and AMC stub functions used exported SDK-style names with capitalized prefixes, for example EXI2_Init, EXI2_EnableInterrupts, EXI2_Poll, EXI2_ReadN, EXI2_WriteN, EXI2_Reserve, EXI2_Unreserve, and AMC_IsStub.

Some functions in DebuggerDriver remained address-based placeholder names such as func_8032AFE4 and func_8032B27C in the diff excerpt, so the claim that every function was named has at least limited excerpt-level ambiguity.
File naming preserved mixed historical/vendor casing such as AmcExi2Stubs.s, DebuggerDriver.s, main_TRK.s, and mem_TRK.s rather than forcing a single project-wide style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced one giant assembly unit with many file-level assembly units while preserving section declarations and global labels.
Used .include "macros.inc" and .section .text consistently in newly split assembly files.
Kept address-commented instruction listings intact during movement, preserving asmdiff traceability.
Adjusted ldscript.lcf to list the new files so linker order and matching addresses could remain stable.
Recognized FORCEACTIVE and FORCEFILES limitations for at least one stub function and avoided a split that would fail to link.
Preserved explicit branch labels such as lbl_8032B128 and function labels such as func_8032AFE4 where better names were not evidenced in the excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Which DebuggerDriver function was the unsplit stub that would not link with FORCEACTIVE and FORCEFILES?
Compare the new MetroTRK file order in ldscript.lcf against later C decompilation PRs for reusable ordering constraints.
Find later PRs that replace func_8032AFE4 or other remaining DebuggerDriver placeholder labels with real MetroTRK names.
Check whether the corrected MetroTRK file splits became the canonical boundaries for subsequent source conversion.
